meta.description: Personal health and training database — Todd Blackhurst AI coaching system

oura_biology_baselines.notes: Oura Biology personal baselines. VO2 Max 38.9 (Below Average — cardio fitness improvement goal). HRV baseline 32.5 ms (any reading below this signals recovery deficit). Avg sleep 6h 25m/night — chronic mild sleep debt.

nutrition_targets.notes: Targets set 2026-04-11. ~380 kcal/day deficit (between mild and moderate). Protein elevated for anabolic resistance at age 57. Fat raised for hormone support. Recalculate if weight changes ±3 kg or training frequency shifts.

body_composition.notes: Baseline from Bevel 30-day Progress Report (Nov 5 – Nov 24)
End-point from Bevel 30-day Progress Report — marked as Low on fat distribution
Bevel weekly scan (week of Apr 6). Visceral Fat Level 2.0. BF% 16.2%, weight 75.9 kg / 167.3 lbs. Primary VAT reduction target ongoing.

Hume Body Pod full scan, updated Apr 10. Air-displacement plethysmography (higher BF% than BIA/Bevel). Trunk muscle Low — core/anti-rotation priority confirmed. Bilateral arm fat High. Goal weight 165 lbs.

Bevel morning scan Apr 11, 4:29 AM. Weight stable at 166.0 lbs. Body fat 17.4% — excellent control. Lean mass 137.1 lbs (78.1% muscle) — preserved well. Visceral fat Lv 3.0 indicates modest VAT. BMR 1779 kcal baseline for deficit calculations.

Bevel morning scan Apr 12, 6:47 AM. Weight 165.8 lbs — down 0.2 lbs from Apr 11. BF 16.3% stable. Lean mass 138.8 lbs (79.1%). Visceral Fat Lv 2.0 (down from 3.0 on Apr 11 — positive signal). BMR 1779 kcal consistent. Trending toward 165 lb goal.

body_composition.body_fat_note: BIA underestimate — not reliable for body fat tracking; use Bevel and Hume ADP pod scan as primary reference
body_composition.notes: Hume Pod BIA daily scale Apr 12, 6:51 AM. Weight 166.8 lbs. Metabolic Age 42 yrs (Excellent). Health Score 673. Trunk muscle Low (consistent with ADP scan Apr 10). BF 10.2% is a BIA artifact — ADP scan (Apr 10) measured 22.0%. Segmental swings vs Apr 10 are hydration-driven, not real muscle change. Trunk Low rating structurally persistent.

recovery_sleep.notes: Low strain day — Oura. Productive Sleep Debt Recovery noted. Outdoor cycling session same morning.
Sleep Quality 55%, below average. Time in Bed 7h 43m, Asleep 7h 17m. Wind-down 9:20 PM, target bedtime 9:50 PM.
Recovery 58% Normal. Sleep 73% Above normal. Deep Sleep 59m (notable). HR Dip 14%. Sleep Bank -1h 49m. Cardio Load 29 Detraining. Sleep HRV avg 31ms / max 126ms. Lowest HR 56 bpm.
Significant HRV Drop — Recovery crashed to 26% (HRV 19.3 ms, ~20ms below baseline). Despite 1h 6min deep sleep. Sleep started Apr 3 at 10:19 PM. Reduction in training intensity indicated.
Recovery Breakthrough — HRV surged to 51.4 ms (+52% rebound from Apr 4's 26%). Nervous system adapting well to training. Sleep 5h 50m (started Apr 4 at 10:56 PM). Sleep Score 69 FAIR.

Recovery Slide 41% — HRV 36.2 ms (Recovery metric), 25 ms avg overnight. HRV/HR spike after Apr 8 high-strain session. Sleep Score 69 FAIR. 5h 28m asleep / 7h 4m in bed (9:21 PM – 4:25 AM). Efficiency 77%. Deep 38m, REM 65m. HR Dip 15%. Debt -2h 13m.

Resilience Signal — steady 57% recovery despite Apr 10 high-strain session. HR dropped 3.1 bpm. Rise: 6h 48m sleep / 8h 13m in bed (9:18 PM – 5:31 AM), Efficiency 83%, Deep 43m, REM 1h 18m, Latency 22m, Restfulness Good, Timing Optimal, Debt -2h 30m Moderate. AutoSleep: 7h 14m total, REM 2h 6m, Deep 30m, Debt -2h 8m, Sleep Time 9:38 PM. HR Dip 14%. Lowest HR 58. HRV avg 26ms / max 151ms. Avg HR 64. SpO2 95.2%.

Recovery Breakthrough — Oura 68% (Above normal, +12% from yesterday). Rise sleep score 70/GOOD. REM above normal at 2h 28m (Oura) / 1h 2m (Rise). HRV 41.7ms above personal baseline of 32.5ms. Sleep short at 6h 15m–6h 54m. Moderate sleep debt (~2h 12m). Latency 37m — attributed to elevated metabolism from prior workout. Restfulness flagged. Respiratory rate slightly below normal (14.1 rpm) — monitor. SpO2 96.1% normal. Training guidance: Amber-Green, full session OK, HR <122 bpm. | Motra supplement: HRV Stabilizing (7d +1.6ms, 30d -3.8ms), RHR 62.7 bpm Stable, VO2 Max 39.6 Good (+4.9 over 90d), Weight 166.3 lbs (30d high, +0.8 lbs/wk trend). Training status: Amber-Green confirmed.

Volume Spike — nearly doubled 14-day average for single session. Warmup block included Cervical CARS, Thoracic Open Book, Glute Bridge, Band Pull Apart, Ext Shoulder Rotation, Kettlebell Swings. HR stayed mostly Z1-Z2. High chest and hinge focus.
Productive Shape Session — 7,764 kg volume. Cardio stayed within HRV limits. Added 13 pts to Cardio Load. Well-executed session.

Right arm 45 vs left arm 58 confirms shoulder asymmetry. Directly validates structural correction priorities in 20_STRUCTURAL_CORRECTION_PRIORITY_USER.md.

coaching_brief.note: Populated hourly 6am–9pm by the scheduled coaching task. See sections below.

sections.recovery:
🟢 Green — Recovery Breakthrough
• HRV 41.7 ms — 9.2 ms above your 32.5 ms personal baseline. Oura 68% (Above Normal), +12% from yesterday's 57%. This is a genuine recovery window, not a noise blip.
• Sleep 6h 15m–6h 54m. REM above normal at 2h 28m (Oura). Latency 37m — attributed to post-workout metabolism. Moderate sleep debt (~2h 12m) but restfulness flagged; deep sleep was only 30m.
• Respiratory rate 14.1 rpm (slightly below normal) and SpO2 96.1% (normal). RR is a watch item — not actionable today unless tomorrow it drops further alongside HRV.
• Non-obvious: HRV above baseline for 3 consecutive days (Apr 9: 36.2 → Apr 11: 38.8 → Apr 12: 41.7 ms). That streak rarely survives a session over 70 minutes. Green light is real — protect it.
sections.training_direction:
Train — Pull-dominant Upper / Structural Correction
• Pattern due: pull. Apr 10 = upper strength (post Apr 8 chest/hinge spike at 118 min). Apr 12 AM = easy walk. Pull + structural correction is the correct pattern today.
• Floor 3 → Floor 2. Activation on 3, primary work on 2. No floor changes mid-session. Left side leads all unilateral work — every set.
• 65-minute hard cap. Set the timer NOW, before your first Floor 3 movement. Cut Block C if needed — never cut the compound rows. The cap is 65 min, not 70, not 'around 65.'
• HR rule: <122 bpm all session. At 5 PM in Taichung, afternoon heat and post-lunch digestion will push baseline HR up 3–5 bpm above morning. Warm-up 10 min before first working set. If HR doesn't drop below 95 after warm-up, rest another 5 min.
• Accountability: Apr 1 = 95 min, Apr 6 = 96 min, Apr 8 = 118 min, Apr 10 = 77 min. That's 4 of your last 4 strength sessions over the cap. Set. The. Timer.
sections.nutrition:
Fat blown — dinner is the only remaining variable
• Logged: 1,389 kcal / 82g P / 59g C / 97g F. Fat is 27g over the 70g target. Budget is closed for fat at dinner.
• Dinner must deliver: ~600 kcal / 68g protein / 130g carbs / 0g fat. Chicken breast (200g) + 1 cup white rice + steamed broccoli = exact fit.
• If you train at 5–6 PM, eat by 7:30 PM at the latest. Late dinner raises overnight glucose and degrades recovery score — which would end your current 3-day HRV streak.
• Apr 11 had no nutrition logged. Not tracking a day doesn't mean you hit targets — it means the deficit is unverifiable. Log dinner tonight.
• Protein discipline is working: 82g logged by mid-afternoon. Get the remaining 68g at dinner and you'll close the week above target.
sections.progress:
0.8 lbs from goal weight — VO2 Max at personal best
• Weight: 165.8 lbs (Bevel, Apr 12 6:47 AM). Down 0.2 lbs from Apr 11. Goal weight 165.0 lbs — you're 0.8 lbs out. This week is likely.
• Visceral Fat Level: 3.0 → 2.0 from Apr 11 to Apr 12. Primary VAT reduction mission is advancing. Lean mass 138.8 lbs at 79.1% muscle — being preserved despite the deficit. Exactly the goal at 57.
• VO2 Max 39.6 ml/kg/min (Motra) — up +4.9 over 90 days from 34.7 to 39.6. Morning Zone 1 walks are driving this. Don't drop them.
• Win: Consecutive morning walks + consistent logging this week have produced the cleanest recovery data in months.
• Watch: Motra 30-day weight trend projects 169.3 lbs if the current +0.8 lbs/wk trend continues. This is almost certainly a hydration + training load artifact, but verify it by logging every meal this week.
sections.watch_for:
1. If HR at the start of your first working set is above 105 bpm — stop, rest 10 min, and re-check. 5 PM session after afternoon snack in Taichung heat means baseline HR will be elevated. Don't start loaded pulling with HR above 105.
2. If you add fat to dinner (oil, cheese, dressing, nuts) — you'll end the day 35g+ over target. The fix: chicken or tuna only, rice or oats, no oil, no sauce. Fat budget is mathematically closed.
3. If tomorrow's Oura respiratory rate is below 14.0 rpm AND HRV drops below 35 ms — shift Monday's session to active recovery (walk only). One RR flag today is a watch; two in a row paired with HRV drop is a deload signal.

session_plan.session_goal: Build left-side pulling strength and volume, counter right-shoulder dominance, thoracic decompression. Complete within 65 minutes.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
exercises.note: 2–3s pause per rep on the roller. T-spine stiffness is the upstream driver of your shoulder asymmetry. Don't rush this.

exercises.name: Quadruped Thoracic Rotation
exercises.note: Right side rotates naturally further than left — force equal range both sides. Slow, deliberate, exhale into rotation.

exercises.name: Banded Hip Abduction
exercises.note: LEFT side first. Your right glute fires automatically — left needs intentional priming before any unilateral lower work.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.note: Slow 2–3s eccentric. Left scapular retraction cue. Primes infraspinatus and rear delt before rowing.

exercises.name: Side-Lying External Shoulder Rotation
exercises.note: LEFT arm first. Light DB only — this is activation, not loading. Corrects the right-shoulder rotator cuff asymmetry from the AI scan.

blocks.label: Block A — Primary Pull Superset (~18 min)

exercises.name: Single-Arm Cable Row
exercises.note: LEFT arm leads every set. Full scapular retraction pause at end range. No torso rotation — this is your right-side dominance correction anchor for the session.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (wide grip)
exercises.note: Pair with A1 during rest period. Full lat stretch at the top — don't cut range. Initiate with lat depression, not bicep curl.

blocks.label: Block B — Shoulder Corrective + Hinge Superset (~16 min)

exercises.name: Cable Face Pull (rope)
exercises.note: External rotation at end range — pull elbows back and high, hands to temples. Light weight, strict form. This is corrective volume, not a strength exercise.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (DB)
exercises.note: Hips back, neutral spine, soft knee bend. Stop hip flexion at approximately 90° to protect hip OA. Control the eccentric 2–3s.

exercises.name: Pallof Press (cable)
exercises.note: LEFT side first. Anti-rotation is the entire point — if your torso rotates, the weight is too heavy. Core and lateral spine correction in one movement.

exercises.name: Suitcase Carry
exercises.note: LEFT hand leads and heavier if possible. Upright posture — zero lateral lean. Trains glute med, oblique, and quadratus lumborum simultaneously.

exercises.name: Med Ball Floor Slam
exercises.note: Full triple extension up, aggressive hip hinge on the way down. If HR is above 112 bpm coming into this block, extend rest to 90s before starting.

coaching_brief_session_plan_note: session_plan lives inside coaching_brief starting next hourly run. Sample below is for today's Sunday active recovery context.

_session_plan_sample.session_goal: Build left-side pulling strength, counter right shoulder dominance, thoracic decompression.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
exercises.note: 2–3s hold per rep. T-spine stiffness is the upstream driver of your shoulder asymmetry — don't skip this.

exercises.name: Quadruped Thoracic Rotation
exercises.note: Right side will rotate further — force equal range. Slow and deliberate.

exercises.name: Banded Hip Abduction
exercises.note: LEFT side first and more sets if needed. Your right glute fires automatically — the left needs intentional priming.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.note: Focus on LEFT scapular retraction cue. Slow eccentric 2–3s. Prepares infraspinatus before any rowing.

exercises.name: Side-Lying External Shoulder Rotation
exercises.note: Left arm first. Light DB only. This is activation not fatigue — don't load it.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Cable Row
exercises.note: LEFT arm leads every set. Full retraction pause at the top. No torso rotation to compensate. This is your right-side dominance correction anchor.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (wide grip)
exercises.note: Pair with A1 during A1 rest. Focus on full lat stretch at top — don't shorten the ROM.

exercises.name: Face Pull (cable, rope)
exercises.note: External rotation at end range. LEFT side pulling slightly harder is the goal. High rep, light weight — this is corrective volume, not loading.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (DB)
exercises.note: Hip hinge — hips back, neutral spine, slight knee bend. Does NOT aggravate OA if hip flexion stays above 90°. Stop before lumbar flexion.

exercises.name: Pallof Press (cable)
exercises.note: Anti-rotation is the primary goal. Don't let your torso twist. LEFT side first — your right-side dominance shows up here too.

exercises.name: Suitcase Carry
exercises.note: LEFT side first and heavier if possible. Upright posture, don't lean away from the weight. This hits everything: glute med, oblique, anti-lateral flexion.

exercises.name: Med Ball Floor Slam
exercises.note: Engagement element. Full triple extension, aggressive hip hinge on the way down. Keep HR in check — if you're above 112 coming into this, rest longer first.
